perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure, Non-Competition, and Non-Solicitation Agreement (“ Agreement ”) is entered into effective as of April 1, 2004 (the “ Effective Date ”), by and between Level 3 Communications, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (“Level 3” or the “Buyer”), on the one hand, and ICG Communications, Inc. (the “Parent”), a Delaware corporation, and ICG Telecom Group, Inc. (the “Company”), a Delaware corporation, and for and on behalf of any of their direct or indirect parents, subsidiaries, successors, or Affiliates (collectively referred to as “ICG” or the “Sellers”), on the other hand.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure, Non-Competition, and Non-Solicitation Agreement (“ Agreement ”) is entered into effective as of April 1, 2004 (the “ Effective Date ”), by and between Level 3 Communications, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (“Level 3” or the “Client”), on the one hand, and ICG Communications, Inc. (the “Parent”), a Delaware corporation, and ICG Telecom Group, Inc. (the “Company”), a Delaware corporation, and for and on behalf of any of their direct or indirect parents, subsidiaries, successors, or Affiliates (collectively referred to as “ICG” or the “Sellers”), on the other hand.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Buyer" is replaced with "Client" in the initial definition. This creates confusion as the contract continues to use "Buyer" throughout, leading to uncertainty about whether "Buyer" and "Client" refer to the same entity. This is especially relevant in sections detailing obligations and rights of the Buyer, creating in-text contradiction on who has the right to enforce the legal text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any breach of any of the covenants set forth in this Article 1 would result in irreparable damage to the Buyer. Consequently, and without limiting other remedies which may exist for a breach of this Agreement, the Buyer will have the right to enforce such covenants by obtaining a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction restraining any violation hereof, pending or following a trial on the merits, without posting any bond and in addition to all other remedies available to the Buyer at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Buyer' with 'Client' in the definition section, while the contradicted section refers to the rights and remedies of the 'Buyer'. This creates a direct conflict because it's unclear whether the rights granted to the 'Buyer' also apply to the 'Client'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Sellers acknowledge that the Buyer would not enter into or close the Purchase Agreement without Sellers’ agreement to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and the execution of this Agreement by Sellers is a condition precedent to the Buyer’s obligation to close under the Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sellers acknowledge that the Buyer would not enter into or close the Purchase Agreement without Sellers’ agreement to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and the execution of this Agreement by Sellers is a condition precedent to the Customer’s obligation to close under the Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Buyer" is replaced with "Customer". This creates confusion as the contract continues to use "Buyer" throughout, leading to uncertainty about whether "Buyer" and "Customer" refer to the same entity. This affects the condition precedent, now is the Customer required to close the deal under the Purchase Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOW, THEREFORE , in consideration of the Buyer’s execution and delivery of the Purchase Agreement, the closing thereunder and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows: ARTICLE 1 NON-DISCLOSURE, NON-COMPETITION AND NON-SOLICITATION 1.1 Non-Disclosure . Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Business as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Business or the Acquired Assets; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Business and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Buyer' with 'Customer' in a condition precedent clause. The contradicted text refers to the 'Buyer's execution' of the agreement. The change creates a contradiction because the contract now inconsistently uses both 'Buyer' and 'Customer', making it unclear which entity's actions are required for the condition precedent to be satisfied, leading to uncertainty about the contract's enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Business as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Business or the Acquired Assets; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Business and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sellers shall not disclose or appropriate for their own use, or for the use of any third party, at any time, any trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information included in or related to the Acquired Assets or the Enterprise as previously operated by Sellers, whether or not developed by Sellers including, without limitation, information pertaining to the customers, vendors, prices, profits, contract terms or operating procedures of Sellers relating to the Enterprise or the Acquired Assets; provided, however, that Sellers may use any such trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (a) if and to the extent used in their businesses other than the Enterprise and (b) in connection with providing services pursuant to the Transition Services Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Business" is replaced with "Enterprise" when referencing sellers information. This creates ambiguity since the document continues to use the term 'Business' elsewhere. Now, the business information is related to "Enterprise", making unsure if both are different or the same. It affects the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply only for a one-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business. Because Sellers’ Business is nationwide, Sellers agree that they will not so compete anywhere in the United States (the “ Territory ”). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sellers may, during the 60-day period following the Effective Date, provide services in the Business to those customers delineated on Schedule 1.02(c) of the Purchase Agreement, subject to and in accordance with Section 5.08 of the Purchase Agreement; provided such services shall be provided solely pursuant to the customer contracts listed on such Schedule 1.02(c) and the amount of such service shall not be materially greater than that provided to such customers by Sellers during the 60-day period prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Business' with 'Enterprise' in the non-disclosure agreement, while the 'Restrictive Period' section continues to use 'Business'. According to the explanation provided, the modification creates ambiguity regarding whether 'Business' and 'Enterprise' are the same, which directly impacts the scope of the non-compete clause in the contradicted text. This misalignment of terminology, as per the definition, leads to a strong in-text contradiction.
